source_jobs.title: Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
As a Data Engineer at Regard, you will help build and maintain the data pipelines and infrastructure that turn raw data into the metrics and insights that drive our product decisions and research. We run an engineering-first stack that prioritizes transparent, code-driven systems over black-box services, and you will contribute to the continued growth and reliability of our data platform.

Working closely with Engineering and Product teams, you'll develop and improve data pipelines, support analytics and machine learning initiatives, and help ensure the quality and availability of critical datasets. You'll have the opportunity to work across the full data stack while growing your expertise in distributed data processing, data modeling, and platform operations.

 

About Regard

Our mission is to bring world-class healthcare to everyone. Regard is an AI-powered Proactive Documentation platform that advances how care is delivered by reviewing all patient data in the EHR to recommend diagnoses and surface clinical evidence. Regard drafts a note even before the physician sees the patient, enabling an approach that gets  documentation right at the point of care - we call it Proactive Documentation. This improves quality of care, reduces physician burden, and improves hospital finances. We are excited by challenges, mission-oriented work, and meaningful relationships. We work closely with some of the top health systems in the country and are leading the change that healthcare - one of the largest and most inefficient industries in the world - needs. We want you to join us.



Our Tech Stack:

 - Data: S3, Apache Iceberg, EMR, PySpark, Dagster, Kubernetes, Clickhouse, PostgreSQL, FastAPI, Metabase

 

Responsibilities:

 - Build and maintain data pipelines that support analytics, machine learning development, and research initiatives

 - Develop and improve data models and transformations that reliably deliver data to downstream consumers

 - Partner with engineering teams to identify and resolve data quality issues, helping ensure datasets are accurate and trustworthy

 - Support the operation, monitoring, and maintenance of the data platform and its pipelines

 - Collaborate with Product, Engineering, and Research teams to deliver data and insights that inform business and product decisions

 - Investigate pipeline failures, data inconsistencies, and upstream changes, contributing to timely resolution and continuous improvement

 - Help optimize data processing workloads and storage patterns to improve performance, scalability, and cost efficiency
   
   

Minimum Qualifications:

 - BS in Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, a related field, or equivalent practical experience

 - 3+ years of experience in data engineering role

 - Experience building and maintaining data pipelines and data models in a production environment

 - Proficiency in Python and SQL

 - Experience working with distributed data processing frameworks such as PySpark

 - Experience with cloud-based data platforms and services (AWS preferred)

 - Practical experience with LLM-assisted development, with an understanding of its capabilities and limitations

 - Willingness to participate in on-call operational support for owned systems



Preferred Qualifications:

 - Experience with one or more of the following technologies: Apache Iceberg, AWS Athena, Dagster, Clickhouse, PostgreSQL, FastAPI, or Metabase

 - Experience supporting data quality, monitoring, and observability initiatives

 - Familiarity with healthcare data, including HIPAA compliance, de-identification, or healthcare data standards such as OMOP CDM

 - Experience building or supporting data pipelines used for machine learning training, evaluation, or production workflows

 - Experience working with cross-functional teams in a fast-paced startup environment



Hybrid Work | Location | Work Authorization

 - For this role, Regard is currently only considering candidates who are authorized to

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
As a Full Stack Software Engineer at Regard, you’ll be involved in all stages of the product development and deployment lifecycle: idea generation, planning, design, prototyping, execution, deployment, and iteration of new and existing features. You’ll collaborate closely with engineering, design, and product teams to bring new features and products to life.

 

Your primary objective will be to develop high-quality software solutions that meet user needs and align with the organization’s business goals. This includes analyzing requirements, designing robust architectures, writing efficient code, and conducting thorough testing.

 

You’ll be responsible for contributing to high-priority projects and continuous improvement initiatives. By delivering innovative and reliable software, you’ll help drive the success of our organization and make a meaningful impact on the industry.

 

About Regard

Our mission is to bring world-class healthcare to everyone. Regard is an AI-powered Proactive Documentation platform that advances how care is delivered by reviewing all patient data in the EHR to recommend diagnoses and surface clinical evidence. Regard drafts a note even before the physician sees the patient, enabling an approach that gets  documentation right at the point of care - we call it Proactive Documentation. This improves quality of care, reduces physician burden, and improves hospital finances. We are excited by challenges, mission-oriented work, and meaningful relationships. We work closely with some of the top health systems in the country and are leading the change that healthcare - one of the largest and most inefficient industries in the world - needs. We want you to join us.



Our Tech Stack:

 - Frontend: TypeScript, React

 - Backend: Python (FastAPI, Flask, Celery), Go, PostgreSQL, Redis

 - Infrastructure & DevOps: Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, GitLab CI/CD

 - APIs & AI: OpenAPI
   
   

Responsibilities:

 - Collaborate with product and design team members to design medium to large projects balancing speed to market, user experience, business goals, and technical architecture quality

 - Operate independently in building new features and capabilities, with support from senior engineers

 - Maintain and improve the quality of the codebase and tooling in your area of responsibility by proactive fixes and improvements, and through identifying and proposing projects to improve the overall backend codebase

 - Participate in operational activities supporting including on-call, monitoring, and production support

 - Contribute as necessary across the stack (front end, infrastructure, AWS, etc)



Minimum Qualifications:

 - BS in Computer Science or equivalent experience

 - 3+ years of professional experience with full stack software development in one or more of the following coding languages: Python, Typescript, Javascript or Go

 - Able to participate in on-call operational support for your areas of responsibility

 - Able to travel up to 4 weeks a year for company co-working and/or retreat weeks

 - Strong verbal and written communication skills

 - Practical experience leveraging AI coding tools in day-to-day software development (e.g., Cursor, Claude Code, Codex)



Preferred Qualifications:

 - Experience with k8s, AWS services (serverless, etc), OpenAI services

 - Experience in a startup and/or high growth environments

 - Experience working on a mature, enterprise SaaS product

 - Experience working with health technology



Hybrid Work | Location | Work Authorization

 - For this role, Regard is currently only considering candidates who are authorized to work in the US without visa sponsorship, and are within the New York City metro area

 - We expect our Engineers to be in the office on Tuesdays and Thursdays. We also require more frequent in-office work during the onboarding period and team onsite weeks up to once per month

 - We will provide relocation assistance to anyone who does not already reside in th

source_jobs.title: Senior Manager, GTM Operations
source_jobs.description:
We’re looking for a strategic, data-driven and highly analytical Senior Manager, GTM Operations to join our growing team. This role will play a critical part in ensuring our go-to-market motions, data, and processes are built with leading-edge thinking and designed to accelerate growth.

 

As our Senior Manager, GTM Operations you won’t just manage a tech stack – you will be the architect of our growth engine. Working at the intersection of marketing, sales, and cutting-edge AI technology, you will have the autonomy to design, build, and optimize the operational foundation and AI enabled workflows that scales our GTM motions. If you love solving complex data puzzles and are excited to pioneer AI-agent workflows in a high-growth environment, this role is built for you. You are highly curious, love to own and solve complex problems, and have already started leveraging AI systems and workflows to unlock greater effectiveness.

 

About Regard

Our mission is to bring world-class healthcare to everyone. Regard is an AI-powered Proactive Documentation platform that advances how care is delivered by reviewing all patient data in the EHR to recommend diagnoses and surface clinical evidence. Regard drafts a note even before the physician sees the patient, enabling an approach that gets  documentation right at the point of care - we call it Proactive Documentation. This improves quality of care, reduces physician burden, and improves hospital finances. We are excited by challenges, mission-oriented work, and meaningful relationships. We work closely with some of the top health systems in the country and are leading the change that healthcare - one of the largest and most inefficient industries in the world - needs. We want you to join us.



Responsibilities:

 - Own the vision for the marketing tech stack, processes, and workflows that support growth in an account-based environment

 - Support implementation of account-based marketing workflows, specifically signal-based inbound and outbound demand generation 

 - Drive alignment between sales and marketing through processes for account prioritization, account/lead scoring and management, and related SLAs

 - Collaborate with Sales/BD to support outbound prospecting workflows and cadences

 - Identify and remove friction in the funnel-tracking conversion from lead to closed-won—and recommend process improvements

 - Build AI-enabled and AI agent workflows to enhance our GTM efforts and enable the entire team to adopt AI in marketing

 - Create and maintain reports and analytics that provide visibility into the marketing funnel and account engagement/penetration



Qualifications:

 - 5+ years in Marketing Ops or a similar role, with experience in a B2B SaaS environment

 - Deep understanding of marketing automation solutions (e.g. Hubspot, Pardot/Salesforce), Salesforce CRM and ABM tools (ideally 6sense)

 - Passionate about finding smarter, more automated ways to do things and have built AI-enabled workflows or AI systems

 - Fluent in funnel metrics, pipeline stages, and campaign performance analysis

 - Thrive in cross-functional collaboration and can speak both “sales” and “marketing”

 - Comfortable diving into CRM and marketing automation logic, and capable of troubleshooting complex workflows



Preferred Qualifications:

 - Strong technical skills

 - Highly analytical and structured thinker

 - Strong understanding of website and digital marketing

 - Experience with sales outreach tools (e.g. Salesloft, Gong, Outreach) and other enrichment tools (e.g. Clay, Keyplay etc.)



Hybrid Work | Location | Work Authorization

 - For this role, Regard is currently only considering candidates who are authorized to work in the US without visa sponsorship, and are within the New York City metro area

 - Our expectation for this role is to be in the office 2 days per week. We also require more frequent in-office work during the onboarding period and team onsite weeks up to once per mont

source_jobs.title: VP of Engineering
source_jobs.description:
As VP of Engineering, you will lead and scale a high-performing software engineering organization, playing a pivotal role in shaping our technology and provider-facing platform. Reporting directly to the CTO, you will drive technical decision-making, guide cross-functional prioritization, and partner closely with product and senior leadership. You will champion engineering excellence, mentor and develop engineering managers, and lead the integration of cutting-edge generative AI technologies to unlock next-generation capabilities for our users.



About Regard

Our mission is to bring world-class healthcare to everyone. Regard is an AI-powered Proactive Documentation platform that advances how care is delivered by reviewing all patient data in the EHR to recommend diagnoses and surface clinical evidence. Regard drafts a note even before the physician sees the patient, enabling an approach that gets documentation right at the point of care - we call it Proactive Documentation. This improves quality of care, reduces physician burden, and improves hospital finances. We are excited by challenges, mission-oriented work, and meaningful relationships. We work closely with some of the top health systems in the country and are leading the change that healthcare - one of the largest and most inefficient industries in the world - needs. We want you to join us.



Responsibilities:

 - Partner closely with the CTO and Head of Product to align technical execution with company priorities and product roadmap goals

 - Own and optimize engineering delivery processes, driving predictability, improving sprint planning, and sharpening estimation accuracy

 - Design an organizational structure that fosters accountability, eliminates bottlenecks, and improves cross-team collaboration

 - Mentor and upskill engineering managers, helping them grow as leaders and effectively manage their own teams

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Design, Customer Success, and Operations to ensure tight feedback loops and accelerate rapid iteration

 - Define and execute the engineering roadmap, establishing best practices for AI-powered, data-rich, and clinically reliable systems



Minimum Qualifications:

 - BS degree in Computer Science or equivalent practical experience

 - 5+ years of software engineering experience, with 2+ years in senior engineering leadership roles (Director or VP level)

 - Experience scaling engineering organizations to 30+ people within high-growth, dynamic startup environments

 - Experience leveraging generative AI and agentic coding workflows to accelerate the software development lifecycle

 - Proven track record of mentoring, upskilling, and managing engineering managers

 - Demonstrated accountability for overseeing system reliability, scalability, and security across large-scale engineering initiatives

 - Strong technical foundation in cloud infrastructure (e.g., AWS), modern CI/CD practices, and observability frameworks

 - Demonstrated track record of partnering with Product leadership to successfully execute complex roadmaps from conception to completion



Preferred Qualifications:

 - Experience navigating the complexities of health tech or enterprise SaaS environments

 - Experience leading engineering teams building with modern stacks like Python, TypeScript, or Go



Hybrid Work | Location | Work Authorization

 - For this role, Regard is currently only considering candidates who are authorized to work in the US without visa sponsorship, and are within either the New York City metropolitan area

 - Our expectation for this role is to be in the office 3 days per week. We also require more frequent in-office work during the onboarding period and team onsite weeks up to once per month

 - We will provide relocation assistance to anyone who does not already reside in the NYC metropolitan area

 - For those who enjoy working from our Manhattan office, we offer catered lunches and other fun perks

source_jobs.title: Engineering Manager
source_jobs.description:
As an Engineering Manager at Regard, you will manage, lead, and grow squads of Software Engineers. You will play a core role, collaborating across engineering and product to lead the development of key features within our provider-facing product. You will be responsible for setting strategic vision, prioritization decisions, technical execution, and team leadership. You’ll play a key role in exploring and integrating generative AI to unlock new capabilities and elevate the user experience across our platform. This role will report directly to our CTO.



About Regard

Our mission is to bring world-class healthcare to everyone. Regard is an AI-powered Proactive Documentation platform that advances how care is delivered by reviewing all patient data in the EHR to recommend diagnoses and surface clinical evidence. Regard drafts a note even before the physician sees the patient, enabling an approach that gets documentation right at the point of care - we call it Proactive Documentation. This improves quality of care, reduces physician burden, and improves hospital finances. We are excited by challenges, mission-oriented work, and meaningful relationships. We work closely with some of the top health systems in the country and are leading the change that healthcare - one of the largest and most inefficient industries in the world - needs. We want you to join us.



Responsibilities:

 - Manage and support squads of AI-empowered engineers through coaching, feedback, and career development

 - Lead development and delivery of product features, collaborating closely with product and design partners to drive user satisfaction and product adoption

 - Drive technical decisions and contribute to system design, balancing long-term scalability with near-term delivery

 - Support the team in maintaining production reliability and performance, including participation in on-call rotations

 - Contribute to the codebase when needed to unblock the team or gain hands-on understanding of technical challenges

 - Drive execution of complex technical projects, ensuring timely delivery and alignment with business goals

 - Collaborate with stakeholders across engineering, product, and clinical subject matter experts to ensure smooth coordination and communication



Minimum Qualifications:

 - BS degree in Computer Science or equivalent practical experience

 - 6+ years of software engineering experience (primarily Backend or Full-stack), and 2+ years spent leading teams of 5 or more developers

 - Demonstrated proficiency with Python, Go and/or Typescript

 - Experience with AI coding tools and AI agents

 - Proficiency with cloud infrastructure and services (e.g., AWS)

 - Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and observability tools (e.g., GitHub/GitLab, ArgoCD, Datadog, Sentry)

 - Experience working in a dynamic startup and/or high-growth environments



Preferred Qualifications:

 - Experience working in enterprise SaaS environment, or health tech



Hybrid Work | Location | Work Authorization

 - For this role, Regard is currently only considering candidates who are authorized to work in the US without visa sponsorship, and are within the New York City metropolitan area

 - Our expectation for this role is to be in the office 3 days per week. We also require more frequent in-office work during the onboarding period and team onsite weeks up to once per month

 - We will provide relocation assistance to anyone who does not already reside in the NYC metropolitan area

 - For those who enjoy working from our Manhattan office, we offer catered lunches and other fun perks

 - Additionally, hybrid employees have the flexibility to work from locations outside of their home office from up to 6 weeks per year
   
   

Perks & Benefits

 - 99% employer paid health benefits (Medical, Dental, and Vision) + One Medical subscription

 - 18 PTO days/yr + 1 week holiday break

 - Monthly health & wellness budget

 - Company-sponsored team retreat + social events

source_jobs.title: Marketing Event Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
Regard is seeking an Marketing Event Coordinator to support the planning and execution of customer-facing and industry events. This role will help deliver a variety of programs, including customer summits, executive dinners, product launches, webinars, and industry conferences.

Reporting to the Sr. Event Marketing Manager, you'll partner closely with stakeholders across Marketing, Sales, Customer Success, and Leadership to coordinate logistics, manage timelines, and support event execution from planning through post-event follow-up. This is an opportunity to play a key role in creating high-quality event experiences that strengthen customer relationships, elevate Regard's brand, and support business growth.


About Regard:

Our mission is to bring world-class healthcare to everyone. Regard is an AI-powered Proactive Documentation platform that advances how care is delivered by reviewing all patient data in the EHR to recommend diagnoses and surface clinical evidence. Regard drafts a note even before the physician sees the patient, enabling an approach that gets documentation right at the point of care - we call it Proactive Documentation. This improves quality of care, reduces physician burden, and improves hospital finances. We are excited by challenges, mission-oriented work, and meaningful relationships. We work closely with some of the top health systems in the country and are leading the change that healthcare - one of the largest and most inefficient industries in the world - needs. We want you to join us.



Responsibilities:

 - Support the planning and execution of external events, including customer summits, executive dinners, product launches, webinars, and industry conferences

 - Coordinate event logistics, including venue sourcing, vendor management, catering, audiovisual support, registration, shipping, event materials, and attendee communications

 - Maintain project plans, timelines, event calendars, and tracking documents to ensure milestones and deliverables are completed on schedule

 - Partner with stakeholders across Marketing, Sales, Customer Success, and Leadership to coordinate event planning and execution

 - Provide on-site event support, including attendee experience management and issue resolution

 - Support event marketing activities, including invitations, email campaigns, social media promotion, and attendee communications

 - Assist with post-event follow-up, reporting, lead management, budget tracking, and invoice processing



Minimum Qualifications:

 - 1+ year of experience in event coordination or event marketing

 - Experience managing multiple projects, deadlines, and stakeholders simultaneously

 - Strong organizational skills and attention to detail

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills

 - Ability to identify issues, propose solutions, and follow through on execution

 - Ability to work independently and manage priorities in a dynamic environment

 - Willingness to travel 30% to support company events



Preferred Qualifications:

 - Experience supporting B2B SaaS events, conferences, trade shows, or customer programs

 - Experience with event management, marketing automation, CRM, design, or productivity tools such as Salesforce, Cvent, Canva, Figma, or Google Workspace

 - Experience supporting webinars or virtual events

 - Experience working with video production vendors or supporting event production workflows

 - Experience in healthcare, health technology, or another regulated industry



Hybrid Work | Location | Work Authorization

 - For this role, Regard is currently only considering candidates who are authorized to work in the US without visa sponsorship, and are within the New York City metro area

 - Our expectation for this role is to be in the office 2 days per week. We also require more frequent in-office work during the onboarding period and team onsite weeks up to once per month

 - We prefer hiring people within commuting distance of our NYC office be

source_jobs.title: Executive Director - Ambulatory
source_jobs.description:
We're seeking a strategic, driven, and innovative Executive Director of Ambulatory to lead the zero-to-one sales motion for Regard. Reporting directly to our VP of Sales and working in close partnership with our CEO and President, you will be the foundational sales hire for our outpatient division.

As the first sales leader in this space, you will help develop, iterate, and execute on the first go-to-market strategy for a new vertical. You will leverage Regard's proven success and clinical case studies to bridge the gap between our health system presence and the untapped outpatient domain.

In this role, you will be the primary revenue driver responsible for securing the partnerships and new logos that will define Regard's footprint across large institutions, MSOs, and clinics. By establishing this new vertical, you will directly advance our mission to ensure every patient receives world-class healthcare in the outpatient setting.



About Regard

Our mission is to bring world-class healthcare to everyone. Regard is an AI-powered Proactive Documentation platform that advances how care is delivered by reviewing all patient data in the EHR to recommend diagnoses and surface clinical evidence. Regard drafts a note even before the physician sees the patient, enabling an approach that gets documentation right at the point of care - we call it Proactive Documentation. This improves quality of care, reduces physician burden, and improves hospital finances. We are excited by challenges, mission-oriented work, and meaningful relationships. We work closely with some of the top health systems in the country and are leading the change that healthcare - one of the largest and most inefficient industries in the world - needs. We want you to join us.



Responsibilities:

 - Own the full sales cycle from lead generation to close

 - Identify client challenges and demonstrate Regard’s value proposition

 - Develop expertise in new product features and service benefits

 - Cultivate and strengthen long-term client relationships

 - Collaborate with Customer Success to ensure seamless client launches

 - Report on sales KPIs and pipeline health

 - Develop pitch decks, sales assets, and Go-To-Market motions



Minimum Qualifications:

 - 5+ years in a closing Account Executive role within healthcare AI and SaaS, specifically selling into the ambulatory and outpatient market (Large Medical Groups, MSOs, and/or clinics)

 - Proven experience navigating complex sales cycles, including building champions and influencing executive-level stakeholders

 - Proficient in leveraging AI tools to enhance sales productivity, automate workflows, and optimize outreach

 - Located within 50 miles of a major airport and willing to travel, as needed (25-60% of time)



Location | Work Authorization

 - We have a strong preference for candidates located in the New York City metro area

 - If located in NYC, the expectation for this role is to be in the office 2 days per week. We also require more frequent in-office work during the onboarding period.

 - We will provide relocation assistance to anyone who does not already reside in the NYC metro area

 - For those who enjoy working from our Manhattan office on a more regular basis, we offer catered lunches and other fun perks

 - Additionally, hybrid employees have the flexibility to work from locations outside of their home office from up to 6 weeks per year



Perks & Benefits

 - 99% employer paid health benefits (Medical, Dental, and Vision) + One Medical subscription

 - 18 PTO days/yr + 1 week holiday break

 - Monthly health & wellness budget

 - Company-sponsored team retreat + social events

 - A sabbatical program



Our goal at Regard is to provide and maintain a work environment that fosters mutual respect, professionalism and cooperation. Regard is proud to be an equal opportunity employer that does not discriminate on the basis of actual or perceived race, creed, color, religion, national origin, an

notes: Strong engineering core, AI focus, and high-tech stack. Some GTM roles, but engineering dominates.
